FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, analyzed Years 2, 3, and 4 follow-up data from N=2,749 participants aged 9-10 at baseline

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical longitudinal analysis; causal mediation analysis with gaming duration/problems (Year 2) → caffeine use (Year 3) → sleep outcomes (Year 4)

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed full-length article published in Journal of Behavioral Addictions 14(3):1368-1379, 2025

FT_IC2.evidence: No qualifying phone/SM-specific variable. Exposure is gaming duration and VGAQ (Video Game Addiction Questionnaire); VGAQ is a problematic-use scale measuring gaming severity, not a phone/SM-modality variable per binding clarification (§2.4). No separate phone/SM variable paired with individual-trait outcomes. Gaming alone without explicit mobile/phone modality fails IC2.

notes: Mediation chain: gaming → caffeine → sleep outcomes. Although quantitative estimates reported (β, 95% CI), the qualifying SM/phone variable is absent at FT-IC2. VGAQ and gaming alone do not meet the Step 1 label requirement (phone/SM-specific) nor Step 2 composite requirement (exclusive phone/SM items).

FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, analyzed Years 2-4 data from approximately 7,260 participants with ADHD assessment via KSADS and VGAQ measurement

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis; longitudinal linear mixed-effects model and parallel mediation model examining ADHD diagnosis → psychosocial mediators → VGAQ scores

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed article published in Frontiers in Psychiatry, 2026 (published online 06 March 2026)

FT_IC2.evidence: Primary exposure variable is VGAQ (Video Game Addiction Questionnaire), a problematic-use scale, not a phone/SM-specific variable. VGAQ is classified as an SM/phone variable itself per binding clarification (§2.4, footnote 1), not as a qualifying outcome on the individual-trait side. No separate phone/SM modality variable (e.g., time on phone, time on SM) measured. Gaming addiction scale alone without explicit mobile/phone modality paired with a separately-measured SM/phone variable fails IC2 per binding rule.

notes: Mediation pathways: ADHD → prosocial peer involvement, school involvement, family conflict (Year 3) → VGAQ scores (Year 4). Although ADHD is an individual-trait variable and psychosocial mediators are individual-trait constructs, the primary exposure operationalization is VGAQ (a problematic-use scale), which fails the qualifying SM/phone-variable requirement of FT-IC2.

FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, longitudinal data 2016-2022, participants aged 9-10 at baseline, N=9,474

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical longitudinal analysis; group-based trajectory modeling of screen time/social media patterns; multivariable linear models examining anxiety/depression as outcomes

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed article published in BMC Public Health 26:262, 2026

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1 failure: No per-modality phone/SM-specific estimate found in results locations. Methods state screen time aggregated across "electronic devices (e.g., TV, texting, gaming, social media)" without separate phone/SM breakout in main analyses (p. 3). Step 2 failure: Composite screen time includes TV, unspecified gaming, social media, and other media bundled without breakdown. Per §2.4, multi-device aggregated time including TV and unspecified gaming without separate SM/phone isolation fails IC2. Composite fails the "exclusively phone/SM/digital-socializing items" requirement of Step 2.

notes: Paper examines screen time trajectories and social media use trajectories separately (Table 1, Figure 1), but screen time composite (primary analysis) bundles TV + gaming + social media + other electronic devices without isolation. Social media trajectory analysis is secondary. The primary screen time exposure variable is a multi-activity composite that violates Step 2 composite-content requirement.
